insights.title: GET /health has no auth or rate-limit middleware
insights.rationale: All /todos routes apply requireAuth, but GET /health is completely unprotected and has no middleware at all — asymmetric and potentially abusable as a free probe endpoint.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Add rate-limiting middleware to the GET /health route in src/app.ts.

insights.title: Auth routes POST /auth/register and POST /auth/login have no middleware
insights.rationale: Every /todos route applies requireAuth, but the two auth routes have zero middleware — no rate-limiting, no brute-force protection. Asymmetric and a security gap.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Add rate-limiting middleware to POST /auth/register and POST /auth/login in src/routes/auth.ts.

insights.title: verifyToken called redundantly by both identifyUser and requireAuth
insights.rationale: Both middleware functions independently call verifyToken on every request, duplicating JWT verification work. A single upstream middleware pass with caching would halve the crypto cost.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Wrap verifyToken with request-scoped memoization so identifyUser and requireAuth share one verification result per request.

insights.title: findUserByEmail called from both registerUser and loginUser without caching
insights.rationale: findUserByEmail is a hot read path hit on every login and every registration duplicate-check. No caching layer exists, causing repeated DB round-trips for the same email lookups.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Add a short-TTL cache (e.g. 30 s) around findUserByEmail in userRepo to reduce redundant DB reads.

insights.title: toTodo row-mapper lives in todoRepo.ts alongside query functions
insights.rationale: toTodo is a pure data-transformation function mixed into the data-access file. It should be extracted to a dedicated mapper/transformer module to keep repo files focused on DB I/O.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Extract toTodo into a new src/repos/todoMapper.ts module and import it from todoRepo.ts.

insights.title: signToken and verifyToken co-located with business logic in authService.ts
insights.rationale: JWT signing/verification are infrastructure concerns mixed into the service layer. Extracting them to a dedicated tokenService or jwtUtil module would make authService.ts easier to test and the token strategy swappable.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Extract signToken and verifyToken from authService.ts into a new src/services/tokenService.ts module.

insights.title: todosRouter is a high-fanout coupling hub (3 service calls + 3 route handlers)
insights.rationale: todosRouter directly wires 3 route handlers to 3 service functions and also owns middleware application, making it a coupling hub. Splitting per-resource route files reduces blast radius of changes.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Split todosRouter into separate handler functions per route (getTodosHandler, addTodoHandler, removeTodoHandler) in src/routes/todos.ts.

insights.title: listTodos has no caching despite being the primary read path
insights.rationale: getTodos → listTodos is called on every GET /todos with no cache. For a list that changes infrequently, even a short TTL cache per user would significantly reduce SQLite read load.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Add a per-user short-TTL cache around listTodos in todoService.ts to reduce repeated DB reads on GET /todos.
